test.name: Plan includes explicit fallback_for chain for church record search
test.description: The skill proposes a new plan for q_001 after prior census searches are exhausted. Church records are a logical next source, but Catholic parish records in Schuylkill County (Diocese of Scranton, 1868+) may not be digitized — so the plan must include a FamilySearch item as primary and a county-level diocesan archive as fallback_for it. Tests the Sequencing logic rubric dimension: fallback chains must appear in the plan when a primary source is likely to be unavailable.

input.user_message: Create the next research plan for q_001. We've already done censuses — focus on what comes next, especially church and probate records.

judge_context: The plan must include at least one EXPLICIT fallback_for relationship: an item whose `fallback_for` field references another item's id. The plan for church or probate records in Schuylkill County should have a primary item (e.g., FamilySearch indexed collection) and a fallback item (e.g., diocesan archive or county courthouse) with `fallback_for` pointing to the primary.
Free/indexed sources should precede paid/unindexed sources in sequence numbers. Probate on FamilySearch (free, partially indexed) should come before Ancestry (paid) fallback.
The rationale on the fallback item must explain WHY the primary might fail — e.g., 'Catholic records for pre-1868 parishes may not yet be digitized; Diocese of Scranton Archives holds originals.'
If no item has a populated `fallback_for` field that references another plan item, score Sequencing logic as 1 (fail).
